title: Generate a roadmap with all preferences and see it rendered and saved
description: Verifies a user can create a project, generate an AI roadmap with preferences, see it on the canvas, and confirm it is saved to the project list.

steps.description: Type "Learn Python for data analysis" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Roadmap canvas renders nodes after generation
description: Verifies that after generating a roadmap, the canvas shows a visible roadmap structure (nodes/steps) to the user.

steps.description: Type "Become a front-end developer" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Generate roadmap after selecting skill level preference
description: Verifies selecting a skill level preference influences generation flow and still results in a rendered roadmap.

steps.description: Type "Learn SQL from scratch" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Generate roadmap after selecting time intensity preference
description: Verifies selecting a time intensity preference still generates and renders a roadmap successfully.

steps.description: Type "Prepare for AWS Cloud Practitioner" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Generate roadmap after selecting learning style preference
description: Verifies selecting a learning style preference still results in a generated roadmap rendered on the canvas.

steps.description: Type "Learn statistics for machine learning" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Generate roadmap after selecting goal preference type
description: Verifies selecting a goal preference option (e.g., certification/interview/job-ready) still generates a roadmap successfully.

steps.description: Type "Master Kubernetes" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Roadmap is persisted: verify project appears after returning to the app
description: Verifies that after generating a roadmap, returning to the main app view shows the created project/roadmap still available (auto-saved).

steps.description: Type "Learn Git and GitHub workflows" into the roadmap goal text field
Click on "Projects" (or the project list toggle) in the app navigation
Verify text "Learn Git and GitHub workflows" is visible

title: Unsupported or prohibited goal triggers AI rejection/domain error with edit option
description: Verifies that entering an unsupported/prohibited goal results in a visible rejection/error message and the user can return to edit the prompt.

steps.description: Type "How to hack into someone’s account" into the roadmap goal text field

title: After AI rejection, user edits goal and successfully generates roadmap
description: Verifies that after an AI/domain rejection, the user can modify the goal and proceed to generate a valid roadmap.

steps.description: Type "How to hack into someone’s account" into the roadmap goal text field
Type "Learn cybersecurity fundamentals ethically" into the roadmap goal text field

title: Open canvas and view roadmap graph with minimap and progress indicator
description: Verifies the main canvas loads and shows the node graph along with key workspace UI elements.

title: Select a node to open node detail panel
description: Verifies clicking a roadmap node opens a visible node details panel.

steps.description: Click on a visible roadmap node on the canvas
Verify element "node details panel" is visible

title: Close node detail panel using close control
description: Verifies the node detail panel can be dismissed from the canvas workspace.

steps.description: Click on a visible roadmap node on the canvas
Verify element "node details panel" is visible
Click the "Close" button on the node details panel

title: Zoom in and zoom out using zoom controls
description: Verifies zoom controls are present and usable without losing the canvas context.

steps.description: Click the "Zoom in" control on the canvas
Click the "Zoom out" control on the canvas

title: Mark a node as completed and verify global progress changes
description: Verifies completing a node updates the visible global progress indicator.

steps.description: Click on a visible roadmap node on the canvas
Verify element "node details panel" is visible
Click the "Mark complete" (or "Complete") button in the node details panel

title: Open node detail panel from canvas and verify core tabs are available
description: Verifies that clicking a node on the main canvas opens a node detail panel with the expected tabs visible.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph

title: Switch between tabs in the node detail panel
description: Verifies that the user can switch between Resources, AI Tutor, Notes, and Quiz tabs and see the selected tab content area.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph

title: Open a resource from the Resources tab
description: Verifies that clicking a resource card from the Resources tab opens the resource view.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph
Click on the first visible resource card

title: Resource open is tracked for the node after opening and returning to Resources
description: Verifies that after opening a resource, the UI indicates it has been opened/visited when viewing the node resources again.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph
Click on the first visible resource card
Click on the "Back" control within the resource view

title: Prerequisite awareness: locked quiz shows message and dependencies list
description: Verifies that when prerequisites are not met, the Quiz tab (or quiz action) is locked and shows a prerequisite message with required dependencies listed.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph that appears locked or advanced

title: Prerequisite awareness: locked action remains inaccessible from the node detail panel
description: Verifies that a locked quiz/action cannot be started when prerequisites are unmet and the UI remains in the locked state.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph that appears locked or advanced
Click on the "Start quiz" button (if visible)

title: Node switching updates the node detail content
description: Verifies that selecting a different node updates the node detail panel to show the newly selected node’s information.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph
Click on a different node in the learning graph

title: Resources tab: empty-state handling when no resources exist
description: Verifies that if a node has no resources, the Resources tab shows a clear empty-state message rather than a broken list.

steps.description: Click on a node that appears to have no resource indicators

title: Pass quiz and receive XP, topic marked complete
description: Verifies a user can complete a node quiz, pass with >=80%, receive +100 XP, and see the topic marked complete.

steps.description: Click on a learning node in the graph/canvas
Click on the "Quiz" tab in the node detail panel

title: Complete quiz with passing score and see +100 XP message
description: Verifies that after answering and submitting a quiz with a passing score, the UI shows a pass message and +100 XP reward.

steps.description: Click on a learning node in the graph/canvas
Click on the "Quiz" tab in the node detail panel

title: Submit quiz before timer ends and see results screen
description: Verifies that submitting the quiz before the timer expires shows a results state (pass/fail) instead of staying on the questions.

steps.description: Click on a learning node in the graph/canvas
Click on the "Quiz" tab in the node detail panel
Click on an answer option for the current question

title: Fail quiz and see option to retry
description: Verifies that a failed quiz attempt shows a fail message and offers a retry option.

steps.description: Click on a learning node in the graph/canvas
Click on the "Quiz" tab in the node detail panel

title: Retry after failing starts a new quiz attempt
description: Verifies that selecting retry after a failed attempt returns the user to an active quiz state.

steps.description: Click on a learning node in the graph/canvas
Click on the "Quiz" tab in the node detail panel

title: Passing quiz marks topic as complete in node detail panel
description: Verifies that after passing a quiz, the node/topic is visibly marked complete in the UI.

steps.description: Click on a learning node in the graph/canvas
Click on the "Quiz" tab in the node detail panel
Click on an answer option for the current question

title: Node-level AI Tutor chat sends a question and renders an AI response
description: Verifies a user can open the AI Tutor from a node detail panel, send a question, and see an AI response rendered on screen.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph
Click the "AI Tutor" tab in the node detail panel
Type "Explain this topic in simple terms with a short example." into the chat input field
Verify an assistant/AI response message is visible in the chat thread

title: Node-level chat persists messages after closing and reopening the node panel
description: Verifies chat history remains visible after the user closes the node detail panel and reopens it for the same node.

steps.description: Click on a node in the learning graph
Click the "AI Tutor" tab in the node detail panel
Type "Give me a quick summary and 3 key takeaways." into the chat input field
Verify text "3 key takeaways" is visible in the assistant/AI response
Click the close button on the node detail panel
Click on the same node again in the learning graph
Click the "AI Tutor" tab in the node detail panel
Verify the previously sent user message is visible in the chat thread

title: Project-level AI Tutor chat can be opened and used from the main canvas
description: Verifies the AI Tutor can be opened without selecting a node, a question can be sent, and an answer is shown.

steps.description: Click the "AI Tutor" button/tab from the main canvas (not inside a node panel)
Type "Create a study plan for this roadmap for the next 7 days." into the chat input field
Verify an assistant/AI response message is visible in the chat thread

title: AI response supports markdown-style formatting (lists/headings)
description: Verifies the AI response displays formatted content such as a bulleted list or headings when requested.

steps.description: Click the "AI Tutor" button/tab from the main canvas (not inside a node panel)
Type "Answer using a markdown heading and a bullet list of 5 items." into the chat input field
Verify text "-" is visible in the assistant/AI response
Verify text "#" is visible in the assistant/AI response

title: AI response supports code-style content display
description: Verifies the AI response can include code-like content and it appears visibly in the message area.

steps.description: Click the "AI Tutor" button/tab from the main canvas (not inside a node panel)
Type "Provide a short JavaScript function example and include it in a code block." into the chat input field
Verify text "function" is visible in the assistant/AI response

title: Stop generation control halts an in-progress response
description: Verifies the user can stop an in-progress generation and the UI reflects the stopped state.

steps.description: Click the "AI Tutor" button/tab from the main canvas (not inside a node panel)
Type "Write a very detailed explanation with many steps and examples." into the chat input field

title: Provider error or rate-limit shows a visible error state and offers retry/regenerate
description: Verifies that when an error occurs, the UI shows a clear message and provides a retry/regenerate action to continue.

steps.description: Click the "AI Tutor" button/tab from the main canvas (not inside a node panel)
Type "Generate an extremely long answer with exhaustive details, citations, and multiple alternatives." into the chat input field
Verify either text "rate limit" is visible or text "error" is visible or element "Regenerate" is visible
Verify either an assistant/AI response message is visible or a visible error message is shown in the chat thread

title: View profile stats: XP and level are visible
description: Verifies that the Profile page shows core gamification stats (XP and level) after login.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation

title: View streak stats: current and longest streak are visible
description: Verifies that the Profile page displays both current streak and longest streak information.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation

title: View learning progress: completed topics and quiz stats are visible
description: Verifies that the Profile page shows learning progress sections for completed topics and quiz performance.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation

title: View badges/milestones section on Profile
description: Verifies that the Profile page includes a visible badges or achievements area tied to gamification milestones.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation

title: View certificate trigger messaging on Profile (if available)
description: Verifies that certificate-related milestone messaging (earned or progress to certificate) is visible on the Profile page.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation
Scroll to the bottom of the Profile page

title: Share roadmap: generate and copy a public read-only link from the app canvas
description: Verifies a logged-in user can open the Share action from the project menu and obtain a public link.

title: Public share page renders roadmap in read-only mode
description: Verifies a shared roadmap link opens and the roadmap content is visible without edit controls.

title: Certificate is available at 100% completion and opens certificate modal
description: Verifies that when progress shows 100%, the Download Certificate action is available and opens the certificate modal.

title: Certificate is not available before 100% completion
description: Verifies that when progress is below 100%, the Download Certificate action is not shown or is disabled.

steps.description: Verify text "Download Certificate" is not visible

title: Private or invalid shared roadmap shows 404/permission error on share page
description: Verifies the share page displays an error message for a private or non-existent roadmap share link.

title: Save settings: appearance, AI panel preference, and language persist after confirmation
description: Verifies a user can update multiple settings, save them, see a confirmation, and observe the preferences remain applied after revisiting the page.

steps.description: Click on "Settings" in the main navigation
Click the appearance toggle to switch between Dark and Light mode

title: Save settings: toggle AI panel preference and verify it persists after leaving and returning
description: Verifies the AI panel preference can be toggled, saved, and remains in the selected state after navigating away and back.

steps.description: Click on "Settings" in the main navigation
Verify element "AI panel preference" is visible
Click on "Profile" in the main navigation
Click on "Settings" in the main navigation
Verify element "AI panel preference" is visible

title: Change language and save: confirmation shown and language selection persists
description: Verifies the language can be changed and saved, with a visible confirmation and the chosen language remaining selected on revisit.

steps.description: Click on "Settings" in the main navigation
Select a different option from the Language dropdown
Click on "Billing" in the main navigation
Click on "Settings" in the main navigation

title: Settings save action shows feedback even when no changes are made
description: Verifies clicking Save without changing settings produces a visible, user-friendly result (confirmation or no-changes message) and does not break the page.

steps.description: Click on "Settings" in the main navigation

title: Switch appearance mode and verify the UI reflects the change immediately
description: Verifies toggling dark/light mode has an immediate visible effect on the Settings UI and does not block saving.

steps.description: Click on "Settings" in the main navigation
Click the appearance toggle to switch between Dark and Light mode

title: Billing page: view plan comparison content is visible
description: Verifies the Billing page loads and shows plan/price comparison information to the user.

steps.description: Click on "Billing" in the main navigation

title: Billing upgrade action shows beta notice that payments are not active
description: Verifies clicking Upgrade (or equivalent CTA) presents a beta notice indicating payments are not active.

steps.description: Click on "Billing" in the main navigation

title: Billing start trial action shows beta notice and offers a support contact option
description: Verifies clicking Start Trial (or equivalent CTA) shows a beta notice and displays an option to contact support.

steps.description: Click on "Billing" in the main navigation
Verify text "payments are not active" is visible
